name: Joseph Mullassery
education: Master’s Degree, Electrical Engineering from The University of Texas at Arlington; Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.), Electronics and Communications Engineering from University of Mumbai
headline: Senior Software Engineer @ Microsoft | Firmware Development, Embedded Systems
about:
As a Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft, I lead the end-to-end delivery of innovative and reliable firmware solutions for various devices and product families. With over 10 years of experience in embedded firmware engineering, I have a strong background in designing and developing firmware for 8/16/32 bit platforms, specializing in Microchip, TI, QCC, NXP/STLPC ARM Cortex – M based systems.

I am proficient in C/C++ and Assembly languages, and have expertise in device driver development for various hardware peripherals, such as I2C, SPI, UART, ADC, etc. I have also delivered robust solutions on several RTOS based platforms, such as FreeRTOS, ThreadX, and QNX. Additionally, I have skills in middleware development, including file system, TCP/IP, and Zigbee. I am a certified Scrum Master and have a proven track record in project management and configuration control, using tools such as SVN, CVS, ClearCase, GIT, JIRA, Bamboo, Confluence, and ADO. My mission is to leverage my embedded systems expertise and firmware development skills to create cutting-edge products that enhance user experience and satisfaction.

currentPositions.description:
Leading end-to-end delivery of the solution to determine ABS root causes for deployed in-field devices for better asset management.

Design and porting of key subsystem features and optimizations to existing Firmware architecture deployed across multiple product families[tablet/laptops/wearables(Audio BLE)].

Delivered high impact features and optimizations such as Deadlock Detection, Robust Power State Machine

Leading design of end-to-end  comprehensive User Telemetry solution which enables Leadership team to make data-driven product release decisions.

Key role in interacting with vendor partners [Intel/Qualcomm/ST/NXP et al] to obtain support and drive design decisions for future products. 

Implementation of common, reusable framework  across product families.

experiences.title: Lead Firmware Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Software Development for electrical protection devices on Coldfire5485 Processor using ThreadX, ARM Cortex microcontrollers and QNX.
Develop device drivers for various peripherals and exposure to protocols such as IEEE1588 & IEC61850.

experiences.title: Master Thesis
experiences.jobDescription:
TOPIC : Embedded Data Acquisition Platform for Low Speed Automated surface measurement on Asphalt
Details:
•	Designed a low speed robot car with a wireless interface for control and mounting the measurement apparatus.
•	Interfacing Intel Galileo with 3-axis accelerometer and device driver for the same in Linux.
•	Algorithm implementation for road profiling using the measured data.

experiences.title: Firmware Intern
experiences.jobDescription: Design and development of Power Metering and Control Algorithms on an 8052 based proprietary ASIC for their solar micro-inverter product range.

Development of common Integrated Software Platform for all device families.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer

Development of Wireless backbone using Zigbee protocol stack.

Development of AT command based UART interface for communication with wireless module.
Implementation of MODBUS RTU protocol.
Implementation of customized real time Micro kernel based on a non pre-emptive scheduling algorithm.
Exposure to development in Industrial Automation domain for Circuit Breakers with various current rating.

experiences.jobDescription:

Development of drivers for OLED and LCD.

publications.title: Self Powered Zigbee Devices
publications.summary: In the existing scenario, short range wireless sensor network is being considered the best choice because of its low power consumption and thus the possibility of working on batteries. This is true for End Devices (sensors), but for networking the sensors/nodes, routers are needed which will be continuously powered and thus are not low power consuming devices. Hence there is an opportunity to come up with solutions/designs which uses Green Energy and with reduced overall cost of power and maintenance. In this paper, we have analyzed the possibility of End Devices (Sensors) as well as Routers operating without any external power source. The concept of Green energy (harvesting renewable energy source) has been aptly used as power source of individual sensor and routers.

recommendations.name: Sriram Bhat
recommendations.subtitle: Sr. Director of Engineering
recommendations.context: Joseph worked with Sriram on the same team
recommendations.description: I worked with Sriram on several projects and I must say I was thoroughly impressed by his ability to find order in chaos. In large and complex projects with several moving parts he always drove clarity and was able to communicate and prioritize tasks correctly. He is a very quick learner and has deep technical knowledge and also is passionate about delivering high quality output. Apart from that he is an excellent team player and a pleasure to work with. I strongly recommend Sriram.

recommendations.name: Vivek Kakkar
recommendations.subtitle: Engineering Director, AMD | Director, Microsoft | Sr Director, Cisco | Angel Investor | Startup Mentor | Lean entrepreneurial innovator |
recommendations.description:
I learned a tremendous amount in my stint with Vivek in the audio team. He fostered a very healthy working environment and drove innovation in the team. He provided a platform and encouraged his team to go above and beyond assigned roles and responsibilities. 
He is an excellent mix of deep technical insight, stellar leadership qualities and a proponent of innovation. I strongly recommend Vivek.
